name: Ashwick Manor
subtitle: A flooded causeway, a dead matriarch, and nowhere to run
description: Ashwick Manor is a rain-darkened country estate built around a Jacobean great hall. Its public rooms display generations of wealth while sealed historic service passages and locked cabinets preserve the less flattering history of the family.
isolation_premise: A violent storm has submerged the only causeway and brought down the telephone line. The bridge cannot be crossed safely before first light, leaving everyone at Ashwick contained inside the estate.

great_hall.name: The Great Hall
great_hall.description: A vaulted hall of black oak, ancestral armour and rain-rattled leaded windows. Every principal room opens from it.
great_hall.atmosphere: The family portraits appear to watch every crossing. A grandfather clock measures the silence with judicial patience.

drawing_room.name: The Drawing Room
drawing_room.description: Low lamps warm a room of burgundy velvet, lacquered cabinets and a drinks trolley arranged with geometric precision.
drawing_room.atmosphere: Perfume and woodsmoke compete beneath the sharper scent of brandy.

library.name: The Library
library.description: Two storeys of books surround a reading table, a green-shaded writing desk and a broad stone fireplace.
library.atmosphere: The fire has burned low. One chair faces the room while another has been knocked slightly askew.

study.name: The Estate Study
study.description: A severe office containing the estate ledgers, a partner desk and a wall safe hidden behind a hunting print.
study.atmosphere: Everything has a labelled place, except the papers pushed hurriedly beneath the blotter.

dining_room.name: The Dining Room
dining_room.description: A long table remains laid after dinner beneath a dim crystal chandelier. Silver and glass throw fractured reflections across the walls.
dining_room.atmosphere: The abandoned place settings make the evening feel interrupted rather than concluded.

kitchen.name: The Kitchen
kitchen.description: Copper pans and iron ranges fill a practical room still hot from dinner service. A pantry door stands beyond the preparation table.
kitchen.atmosphere: Steam clouds the windows while the storm drums steadily on the service-yard roof.

gallery.name: The Long Gallery
gallery.description: A narrow gallery of portraits and glass display cases runs along the eastern side of the house.
gallery.atmosphere: Lightning repeatedly turns the painted faces into pale witnesses.

conservatory.name: The Conservatory
conservatory.description: Palms and winter roses crowd a glass room overlooking the flooded lawn. A tiled fountain burbles beneath the rain.
conservatory.atmosphere: Condensation beads on every pane, blurring the outside world into shadow and water.

chapel.name: The Private Chapel
chapel.description: A small stone chapel adjoins the west wing, furnished with dark pews, a brass lectern and a curtained vestry alcove.
chapel.atmosphere: Rainwater ticks against stained glass. The unlit candles smell faintly of honey and smoke.

hall_clock.name: Grandfather clock
hall_clock.description: An eight-day clock maintained by the estate staff.
hall_clock.search_text: The clock is running accurately. Its service card is tucked inside the case.

hall_coat_stand.name: Coat stand
hall_coat_stand.description: A crowded stand of damp coats and umbrellas.
hall_coat_stand.search_text: The coats can be checked for rain, fibres, missing buttons and concealed objects.

drawing_drinks_trolley.name: Drinks trolley
drawing_drinks_trolley.description: Decanters, glasses and a silver ice bucket.
drawing_drinks_trolley.search_text: The arrangement records who poured what, and which glass was left unfinished.

drawing_sofa.name: Velvet sofa
drawing_sofa.description: A deep burgundy sofa facing the fireplace.
drawing_sofa.search_text: Small personal items can slip between the cushions.

drawing_gramophone.name: Gramophone
drawing_gramophone.description: A cabinet gramophone with a stack of dance records.
drawing_gramophone.search_text: The last record played has not been returned to its sleeve.

library_desk.name: Writing desk
library_desk.search_text: The drawers contain correspondence, appointment cards and stationery impressed with the Ashwick crest.

library_fireplace.name: Fireplace and tools
library_fireplace.description: A stone hearth with poker, tongs, brush and ash pan.
library_fireplace.search_text: Soot, ash and the placement of the tools may preserve signs of recent handling.

library_clock.name: Mantel clock
library_clock.description: A small brass carriage clock on the mantel.
library_clock.search_text: Its glass and mechanism can be examined for impact damage and timing.

library_atlas_case.name: Atlas cabinet
library_atlas_case.description: A low locked cabinet of estate maps and oversized volumes.
library_atlas_case.search_text: The cabinet holds old plans, including service routes and ownership boundaries.

study_desk.name: Partner desk
study_desk.description: An orderly desk shared by the estate manager and solicitor when business is conducted at Ashwick.
study_desk.search_text: The blotter, correspondence trays and locked drawers contain current estate business.

study_safe.name: Wall safe
study_safe.description: A combination safe concealed behind a faded hunting print.
study_safe.search_text: The safe contains deeds, trust instruments and sealed personal files.

study_wastebasket.name: Wastepaper basket
study_wastebasket.description: A wire basket lined with yesterday's financial pages.
study_wastebasket.search_text: Torn or discarded papers can be reconstructed if collected carefully.

dining_sideboard.name: Silver sideboard
dining_sideboard.description: A mahogany sideboard holding serving silver and after-dinner bottles.
dining_sideboard.search_text: The inventory card makes missing or recently cleaned objects conspicuous.

dining_table.name: Dining table
dining_table.description: Eight guest places and the head of the table remain set.
dining_table.search_text: Glasses, crumbs, napkins and seating positions preserve the end of dinner.

kitchen_prep_table.name: Preparation table
kitchen_prep_table.description: A scrubbed oak table with a magnetic knife rack above it.
kitchen_prep_table.search_text: The knife ledger and cutting boards reveal what was used during service.

kitchen_stove.name: Iron range
kitchen_stove.description: A broad range banked down after dinner.
kitchen_stove.search_text: The ash and hot plates could have been used to burn or heat a small item.

kitchen_bin.name: Kitchen bin
kitchen_bin.description: A lidded bin of dinner scraps and trimmings.
kitchen_bin.search_text: Discarded ingredients and stained cloths can explain marks that appear sinister elsewhere.

kitchen_pantry.name: Pantry
kitchen_pantry.description: Shelves of preserves, dry goods and household stores.
kitchen_pantry.search_text: The pantry inventory includes cleaning agents, medicines and cooking extracts.

gallery_display_case.name: Bronze display case
gallery_display_case.description: A glass case of small bronzes and military keepsakes.
gallery_display_case.search_text: Dust outlines make any recently moved object visible.

gallery_portrait.name: Ashford family portrait
gallery_portrait.description: A monumental portrait in a deep gilt frame.
gallery_portrait.search_text: The frame and wall cavity provide a narrow hiding place.

gallery_window.name: East window
gallery_window.description: A tall casement overlooking the flooded lawn.
gallery_window.search_text: The latch, sill and condensation show whether it was opened during the storm.

conservatory_bench.name: Potting bench
conservatory_bench.description: A bench of tools, twine and labelled seed envelopes.
conservatory_bench.search_text: Mud and cuttings can be compared with traces carried into the house.

conservatory_fountain.name: Tiled fountain
conservatory_fountain.description: A shallow decorative fountain circulating cold water.
conservatory_fountain.search_text: The basin could rinse a hand-sized object but would retain fibres in its grate.

conservatory_door.name: Garden door
conservatory_door.description: A bolted glass door to the terrace.
conservatory_door.search_text: The bolt, threshold and mud confirm whether anyone used the door after the rain began.

chapel_altar.name: Stone altar
chapel_altar.description: A plain altar with two heavy brass candlesticks.
chapel_altar.search_text: Wax, dust and polished brass preserve handling marks.

chapel_vestry.name: Vestry cabinet
chapel_vestry.description: A curtained oak cabinet of linens and parish records.
chapel_vestry.search_text: The deep lower shelf is large enough to conceal papers or a small object.

chapel_pews.name: Pews
chapel_pews.description: Six rows of dark oak pews with narrow prayer-book racks.
chapel_pews.search_text: The racks and undersides can hold dropped or deliberately hidden items.

slot_library_fireplace.description: among the fireplace tools and ash

slot_library_clock.description: on and inside the mantel clock

slot_kitchen_table.description: on the preparation table and knife rack

library_poker.name: Iron fireplace poker
library_poker.description: A long square-shafted poker heavy enough to cause fatal blunt trauma.

gallery_bronze.name: Bronze horse statuette
gallery_bronze.description: A dense bronze figure with a weighted stone base.

kitchen_knife.name: Chef's knife
kitchen_knife.description: A broad professional knife recorded in the kitchen inventory.

chapel_candlestick.name: Brass candlestick
chapel_candlestick.description: A heavy altar candlestick polished weekly by the housekeeper.

estate_key.name: Estate cabinet key
estate_key.description: A small brass key for the manor's map and document cabinets.

safe_combination.name: Safe combination note
safe_combination.description: A private mnemonic that provides the estate study safe combination.

murder_opportunity_rules.access_summary: The killer and victim must share the room during a schedule gap, and the selected weapon must be accessible there or carried through a valid route.
murder_opportunity_rules.witness_risk: Anyone sharing the room or an explicitly modeled observer on the route invalidates an unseen attack unless the case accounts for that witness.

murder_opportunity_rules.access_summary: The knife must leave the inventoried rack and the killer must have a feasible route from the kitchen to the murder room.
murder_opportunity_rules.witness_risk: Kitchen staff can establish when the knife was last present, creating a testimonial trace.

movement_constraints: The flooded causeway prevents anyone leaving the estate before dawn.
The terrace and garden are unsafe during the storm and are not playable rooms in the MVP.
NPC and player movement must follow a listed door unless an explicit event changes access.

events.name: Storm intensifies
events.description: A squall strikes the east windows and briefly dims the electric lights.

events.name: Causeway update
events.description: The water continues to rise; no vehicle can reach the house before morning.
